decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses time-scaled Bayesian phylodynamic analysis and phylogeographic reconstruction to infer DBTV introduction into South Korea and subsequent regional dissemination.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application of phylogeographic reconstruction to DBTV circulation and spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes mitochondrial COI sequences from 17 countries to characterize phylogeographic patterns and population connectivity in avian Isospora.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogeographic application to an avian parasite.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian phylogeographic reconstruction of 1,004 genomes to trace the international dissemination histories of G. parasuis lineages.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic phylogeographic analysis of a swine pathogen.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs phylogeographic analyses that identify France and China as seeding regions in global CVA4 circulation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete viral phylogeographic application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses discrete phylogeographic reconstruction with Bayes-factor support to infer CHIKV spatial diffusion pathways and multiple introductions into China.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic-diffusion analysis of chikungunya outbreaks.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses spatial phylogeographic reconstruction to compare centralized PCV2 diffusion with multicentered PCV3 spread in China.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete pathogen-diffusion application using viral sequences.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses genetic and distributional data to examine the phylogeography and geographic ranges of Asian Amblyomma tick lineages.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogeographic application to medically important tick vectors.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogenetic and genomic analyses to assess phylogeographic structure, inter-provincial mixing, and cross-regional transmission of L. monocytogenes CC87.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic analysis of pathogen geographic circulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogeographic reconstruction to estimate WNV migration routes, introduction dates, and viral diffusion rates in Russia.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogeographic reconstruction of viral introductions and spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogeographic reconstructions to infer introductions and spatial dispersion of DENV-3 lineage 3III_B.3.2 in Brazil.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic-diffusion analysis of dengue spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogeographic reconstruction to identify putative transmission nodes and broad geographic dissemination of porcine sapelovirus lineages.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogeographic application to porcine sapelovirus.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogeographic analyses to infer a single OROV introduction into Cuba followed by within-country spread and secondary transmission hubs.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogeographic analysis of the 2024 Cuban outbreak.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies phylogenetic and phylogeographic analyses to characterize co-circulating OROV lineages in the Colombian Amazon.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete pathogen phylogeographic application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents a population-structure-aware approach for classifying pangenome genes by within-species distribution while accounting for lineage structure and uneven sampling.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological source introducing and demonstrating the population-structure-aware approach.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes leveraging the distributed Laboratory Response Network model to build laboratory capacity under the Global Health Security Agenda.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit guidance proposing a public-health laboratory implementation-network model.
named_source_mentions: WHO Revised International Health Regulations (IHR) of 2005
supported_label_variants: Laboratory Response Network for Biological Threats Preparedness (LRN-B)

decisions.relationship_evidence: Implements an organizational and communication network involving clinicians, media, expanded clinics, and monitoring to increase HPV vaccination coverage.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete implementation-network application for vaccination delivery.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Formulates and analyzes a delayed SIR epidemic model combining nonlinear incidence, spatial diffusion, and travelling-wave behavior.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological analysis of a reaction-diffusion epidemic-model variant.
supported_label_variants: SIR epidemic model with nonlinear incidence rate, spatial diffusion and time delay

decisions.relationship_evidence: Investigates stationary pattern dynamics in a spatial epidemic model with logistic growth using amplitude-equation analysis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Methodological source analyzing the dynamics of a spatially diffusive epidemic model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a multiscale transmission model that links individual movement and pathogen diffusion to population-level disease dynamics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit model-development source coupling diffusion with epidemic transmission across scales.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Constructs a novel multi-group SEIR epidemic model incorporating age structure and spatial diffusion and derives its global dynamics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development of a spatially diffusive SEIR model.
supported_label_variants: multi-group SEIR epidemic model with both age structure and spatial diffusion

decisions.relationship_evidence: Extends a polio compartmental model to spatial diffusion and proposes a positivity-preserving finite-difference solution method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development and numerical treatment of a spatial-diffusion epidemic model.
supported_label_variants: mathematical model of polio propagation with spatial diffusion

decisions.relationship_evidence: Transforms an age-space foot-and-mouth disease model into a diffusive epidemic model with nonlocal infection coupling and vaccination.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological construction of a diffusive epidemic model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Investigates well-posedness and steady-state stability for an infection-age compartmental epidemic model whose movement is governed by spatial diffusion.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological analysis of a spatial-diffusion epidemic model.
supported_label_variants: epidemic model with infection age and spatial diffusion

decisions.relationship_evidence: Establishes a stochastic reaction-diffusion model for Zika transmission and extends it with optimal-control interventions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a stochastic reaction-diffusion epidemic model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Establishes and analyzes a stochastic avian-influenza model incorporating spatial diffusion and nonlocal delay.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development of a spatially diffusive stochastic epidemic model.
supported_label_variants: stochastic avian influenza model with spatial diffusion and nonlocal delay

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies nonparametrically estimated Hawkes point processes to forecast the 2018–2019 DRC Ebola outbreak and evaluates predictions against observed outbreak sizes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete near-real-time application of Hawkes models to an Ebola outbreak.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a time-modulated Hawkes-process epidemic model incorporating time-varying countermeasures, detection, and heterogeneous infections.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological introduction of a time-modulated Hawkes epidemic model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes semi-mechanistic Hawkes processes for reconstructing, forecasting, and separating imported from local malaria transmission.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological proposal and demonstration of Hawkes processes for epidemiology.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops Hawkes transmission models with spatiotemporal covariates and validates their COVID-19 forecasting performance against competing models.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development and validation of spatially covariate-adjusted Hawkes models.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a phylogenetic Hawkes process linking pathogen evolution to spatial contagion and supplies parallel likelihood implementations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development of a biologically modulated spatial Hawkes model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates nonparametric Hawkes forecasting on Ebola data against SEIR models and quantifies its comparative prediction error.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit benchmark of Hawkes-model epidemic forecasting performance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces a latent Hawkes epidemic model with temporal covariates and a Kernel Density Particle Filter for latent-case and reproduction-number inference.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological introduction and benchmark of a latent Hawkes model.
supported_label_variants: latent Hawkes process with temporal covariates

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper proposes a spatial Poisson process for latent HIV transmission statuses; the title and abstract do not identify a Hawkes or other self-exciting process.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit self-exciting-process lineage lead.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Extends an epidemic model to age strata using a latent marked Hawkes process and particle-filter inference.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological extension of a latent Hawkes epidemic model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates how accurately nonparametric Hawkes models forecast COVID-19 surges and reports classifier performance across false-alarm thresholds.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit performance evaluation of Hawkes-based surge forecasts.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces the Directed Weighted All Nearest Neighbors network for sequence data and benchmarks its structure, resilience, and construction speed.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development and validation of a sequence-similarity network representation.
supported_label_variants: Directed Weighted All Nearest Neighbors (DiWANN) network

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops between-region and within-region models of continental Schmallenberg-virus spread and compares them with microsimulation approaches.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development and methodological discussion of spatial spread models.
supported_label_variants: model for the transmission of SBV between regions

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and simulation-tests an approach for estimating mean transmission distance from case locations, onset dates, and generation times.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological source for estimating spatial disease dispersal.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a mechanistic cholera-transmission model estimating spatial variability in reproduction numbers, environmental seeding, and reporting rates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a spatial transmission model with a concrete cholera application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a kernel-modulated SIR model to represent COVID-19 spread across county-to-continental spatial scales.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development of a multiscale spatial spread model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes and analyzes an epidemic model with nonlocal dispersal and pollution-dependent transmission.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development of a nonlocal spatial-dispersal epidemic model.
supported_label_variants: epidemic model of nonlocal dispersal with air pollution

decisions.relationship_evidence: Calibrates a stochastic SEIRD ensemble and linked spillover-hazard model to project outbreak spread and cross-border importation risks.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete spatial-spillover modelling application to a Bundibugyo-virus outbreak.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops an importation simulation and global risk framework to identify locations with likely cryptic Zika spread.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a spatial framework for estimating unobserved pathogen spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies a Bayesian hierarchical model across Brazilian microregions to estimate and map unreported leprosy cases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete spatial hidden-burden application to leprosy surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Creates a high-resolution Bayesian spatial model integrating incidence, notifications, and diagnostic data to estimate undiagnosed TB hotspots.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit model-development source for spatially predicting hidden TB burden.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses GIS kriging to map and compare the spatial diffusion patterns of norovirus epidemics across three seasons.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete geostatistical application to norovirus surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies a Bayesian conditional autoregressive spatiotemporal model to estimate meteorological associations with COVID-19 incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete spatial-regression application to COVID-19 case data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Fits Bayesian geostatistical models to serosurvey data and generates high-resolution HEV seroprevalence maps.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete geostatistical disease-mapping application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes and validates a two-step spatial INLA framework for estimating local reproductive numbers in regions with sparse or missing data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development and validation of a spatial-estimation framework.
supported_label_variants: Bayesian Integrated Nested Laplace Approximation (INLA) spatial model

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses generalized linear models with environmental predictors; spatial heterogeneity in NDVI is a covariate rather than an explicit spatial-regression or geostatistical model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit spatial-regression or geostatistical lineage lead.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses cluster analysis, standard-deviation ellipses, GeoDetector, and a meteorological generalized additive model, but does not explicitly apply a spatial-regression or geostatistical model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The stated methods belong to neighboring spatial-analysis categories rather than the exact concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reports spatial hotspots and possible environmental drivers, but the abstract does not identify a spatial-regression or geostatistical model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit exact-method application or source.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses spatial statistical models on gridded surveillance data to estimate changes in African cholera incidence and burden.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete spatial-statistical disease-burden application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a framework comparing reported cases with healthcare-worker infections to assess underreporting effects on COVID-19 spatiotemporal distributions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit framework development for spatial and temporal reporting heterogeneity.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a Bayesian hierarchical spatial model that corrects count data for underreporting, overreporting, and spatial dependence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological source for spatial misreporting correction.
supported_label_variants: Bayesian Hierarchical Spatial Model to Correct for Misreporting in Count Data

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies a Bayesian spatial framework and prevalence-to-notification ratios to map subnational TB underdiagnosis and underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete spatial reporting-gap analysis for TB surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a two-tiered Bayesian hierarchical model to estimate spatially and temporally varying true COVID-19 incidence and detection rates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a spatiotemporal underreporting model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian model averaging with underreporting-error correction and spatial effects to estimate regional temperature–COVID-19 associations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete analysis incorporating reporting error and spatial dependence.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents Bayesian hierarchical spatiotemporal disease-mapping models for joint prediction of COVID-19 infection risks and reporting rates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological presentation of spatiotemporal underreporting models.
supported_label_variants: Bayesian hierarchical spatiotemporal disease mapping models

decisions.relationship_evidence: Maps subnational HAT burden in Uganda while incorporating sensitivity to underreporting and spatial scale.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete spatial burden-mapping application adjusted for underreporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a modified distance-sampling framework to map infectious-disease incidence while correcting distance-related case underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Provides both the methodological framework and a self-contained R Shiny mapping implementation.
supported_label_variants: modified version of distance sampling for prediction of infectious disease incidence

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a local spatiotemporal incidence-estimation method that adjusts passive malaria surveillance for diagnostic stock-outs and healthcare access.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development for spatial risk estimation under incomplete reporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces and validates an epidemic risk-index methodology whose external validation uses distance sampling to correct geographically patterned underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological source for adjusted spatial epidemic-risk mapping.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a Bayesian spatiotemporal occupancy model that maps latent Coccidioides endemicity while accounting for false-positive and false-negative detection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development for spatial risk mapping with imperfect detection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses an ecological niche model that accounts for underreported outbreaks to map foot-and-mouth-disease risk across South Asia.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete adjusted spatial risk-mapping application.
supported_label_variants: ecological niche model (ENM) that accounts for the under-reporting of outbreaks

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops separate ecological-niche occurrence and reporting models to locate environmentally suitable areas with potential HPAI underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of an occurrence-versus-reporting spatial risk framework.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Maps weekly district-level excess mortality to reconstruct the geographic propagation and distinct entry regions of pandemic influenza in Sri Lanka.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete spatial transmission analysis of the 1918–1919 influenza pandemic.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Maps tsetse abundance and trypanosome infection along a transect but does not explicitly analyze pathogen transmission across space or time.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Vector-distribution analysis alone is not an explicit spatial-transmission analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes temporal and spatial rabies distributions, laboratory-attributed infection sources, and interspecies risk associations across South America.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete spatiotemporal and interspecies transmission analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes subdistrict-level spatiotemporal spread, propagation speed, and reproduction-number dynamics during Shanghai's Omicron outbreak.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete spatiotemporal transmission analysis of an outbreak.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogenetic analysis to test geographic structuring of HIV-1 sequences by region, obtaining a null result for geographic structure.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete, explicitly geographic molecular-transmission analysis despite its null spatial finding.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Integrates sequence and case-count data to study temporal epidemic and genetic-diversity dynamics, without an explicit spatial-transmission model or analysis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit relationship to the exact spatial-transmission concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses estimated phylogenies to infer geographic structure, endemic circulation, and lineage migration among locations for enterovirus 71.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogenetic analysis of pathogen spatial structure and migration.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogenetic and Bayesian coalescent analyses to infer cross-country genetic exchange and geographically confined circulation of HIV recombinant lineages.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete molecular analysis of spatial circulation between Argentina and Uruguay.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces a two-tiered phylodynamic simulation model for epidemiological and molecular evolution, but the model described is not spatial.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Methodological source for a neighboring nonspatial phylodynamic concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Studies viral evolutionary trajectories and population dynamics; geographic comparisons are contextual and no spatial-transmission model is identified.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit exact-concept lineage role.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reconstructs a four-person HIV transmission network and event timing, without a spatial component.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Transmission-network reconstruction is a neighboring nonspatial concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses coalescent analysis to study norovirus population expansion and molecular adaptation, not spatial transmission.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit spatial model or geographic-transmission analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Critiques interpretation of coalescent effective population size as the number of infections, but the critique is not specific to a spatial-transmission model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Diagnostic discussion concerns nonspatial phylodynamic inference.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogenetic and Bayesian coalescent methods to determine temporal and spatial dynamics and trace HIV recombinant forms to Yunnan Province.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete molecular analysis of spatial HIV epidemic origins and expansion.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates mutation rates and common-ancestor dates for globally disseminated rotavirus lineages but does not describe a spatial-transmission model or reconstruction.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Worldwide spread is an interpretation rather than an explicit exact-method application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces allele-dynamics plots for evolutionary and selection dynamics; the method does not model spatial transmission.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Method source for a neighboring evolutionary-visualization concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogenetic and phylodynamic analysis to date silent circulation before a Finnish outbreak, without an explicit spatial-transmission model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit exact-concept application or source.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian coalescent phylogenetics on isolates sampled across France to reconstruct geographic epidemic origin and routes of viral diffusion.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete molecular analysis of geographically structured HCV diffusion.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes virus–host codivergence and interspecies transmission, not geographic or spatiotemporal transmission.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Host-association analysis is a neighboring nonspatial concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs molecular surveillance, diversity estimation, and transmission clustering in a Bangkok trial cohort without an explicit spatial-transmission model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit exact-concept lineage role.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Studies genome-scale evolutionary dynamics, reassortment, selection, and population bottlenecks without spatial-transmission analysis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit spatial model or geographic reconstruction.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian phylodynamics and phylogenetic geographic structure to infer mobility-linked spread and multiple introductions between Africa and Italy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete molecular analysis of geographic HIV lineage exchange.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates HCV incidence from cohort data and compares it with earlier phylodynamic findings; it does not itself apply a spatial-transmission method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Citation-level agreement with phylodynamics is not a direct method relationship.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reviews HIV evolution, molecular epidemiology, resistance, and future phylodynamic use without presenting a specific spatial-transmission method or guidance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Broad review content does not establish an exact-concept lineage role.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies phylodynamic methods to influenza selection and temporal population history, without explicitly modelling spatial transmission.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No direct relationship to the exact spatial-transmission concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes phylogenetic transmission clusters and risk behavior but does not include a geographic or spatial-transmission model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Transmission clustering alone is a neighboring nonspatial concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces a particle-MCMC framework for nonlinear phylodynamic inference from genealogies and time series, but the demonstrated SIR model is nonspatial.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Authoritative for nonspatial phylodynamics, not the exact assigned concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Derives equilibrium coalescence rates for epidemiological models without a spatial component.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Methodological contribution concerns nonspatial coalescent epidemiology.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates HIV transmission-network degree parameters from sequences but does not model geographic or spatial transmission.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Network-degree inference is a neighboring nonspatial method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian phylogenetics to distinguish locally acquired from externally acquired infections and reconstruct separate HIV epidemics in Hong Kong.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete molecular analysis of geographic HIV acquisition and spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Studies temporal amino-acid-frequency change and common ancestry in HIV subtype C without a spatial-transmission model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Heterogeneous sequence origins do not constitute an explicit spatial analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies phylodynamic analysis to norovirus evolutionary rates and emergence, not spatial transmission.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit geographic-transmission method or lineage lead.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reviews phylogenetic insights into HIV evolution and dynamics without an explicit spatial-transmission method, application, or critique.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Broad phylogenetic review is not an exact-concept lineage source.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a phylodynamic framework to reconstruct temporal HBV population growth in Albania, without an explicit spatial-transmission component.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No direct relationship to the exact spatial-transmission concept.
